author: judge-correctness-c3

dimensionScores.rationale: Both c2 P1s are genuinely fixed (companies_house.py:196-211 extracts the 8-char number and drops the trailing yyyymmdd; reconcile no longer truncates audited-only periods) and the whole P2 correctness cluster is resolved (F8 price-distillate refresh snapshot.py:262-269, F9 GLEIF weekly service.py:322-329/477-485, F10 coreg skip edgar_fsds.py:90, F12 FX latest-period-only fx.py:91-109, F16 utf-8-sig gleif.py, F15 dropped-symbol snapshot.py:272). BUT the F6 fix shipped a NEW P1: reconcile.py:63 appends audited-only periods UNSORTED, and build_symbol_snapshot writes the current price to price.iloc[-1] (snapshot.py:79) and return_6m to out.iloc[-1] (snapshot.py:96) assuming ascending periods, so both land on the OLD last-appended period — the latest period is left with NaN price/momentum for exactly the deep-history US universe F6 targets (repro runs/regression-price-period.txt: return_6m on 2022 not 2024). Net up from 3.57, capped by the live P1.

221 pytest + 62 vitest green (runs/pytest.txt, runs/vitest.txt) with representative NEW fixtures that fix c2's false-confidence: the real Prod<batch>_<seq>_<number>_<date> filename (test_companies_house.py), a coreg row listed first (test_fsds.py), FX latest-only (test_fx.py), price-distillate refresh (test_fr003_compute.py). Big lift over c2. But test_fr010_esef.py's F6 fixture asserts merged values by LABEL only and never checks period order — precisely the blind spot that let the new P1 regression stay green.

Flat vs c2. The only open item is F5/c3 — mirror.py:102 streams to disk with no byte ceiling and mirror.py:88 uses a per-op (not total) timeout; URLs are hardcoded/trusted so no SSRF. No new exposure from the fixes.

edgar_fsds.py cleanly split into _wanted_adsh/_accumulate/_build_frames and gleif into _parse_isin_lei streaming helpers, but ingest/enrichment.py is still the 617-LOC top hotspot holding 7 run_* cycles (enrichment.py:43-513, F5/c3) — unchanged since c2.

The headline perf win: the F11 OOM is fixed — GLEIF (~1GB decompressed) and FSDS num.txt (100s of MB) now stream row-by-row via TextIOWrapper (gleif.py load_isin_lei_map, edgar_fsds.py iter_fsds) instead of whole-file reads, restoring the mirror's memory-safe design on the 1GB-VPS target.

topFindings: F1 (P1, correctness): the c2 F6 fix reindexes audited-only periods at the END without re-sorting (reconcile.py:63); build_symbol_snapshot assigns the current price (snapshot.py:79) and return_6m (snapshot.py:96) to iloc[-1], so for scraped symbols with deep FSDS/EDGAR history both land on an OLD period and the latest period is mis-priced — the flagship deep-history universe is silently corrupted (repro runs/regression-price-period.txt).
10 c2 defects verified fixed at file:line (F6/F7 P1s + F8/F9/F10/F11/F12/F16 P2s + F4-edinet/F2-mirror opportunities), plus F15 incidentally — a real recovery from 73.
Residual P2 correctness: F2/c3 audited-only symbols still get an empty audited_fields column (snapshot.py:199), F3/F4 EDINET still book interim/parent-only figures (opt-in).

author: judge-pipeline-c3

dimensionScores.rationale: The production ingest/compute path is materially better: incremental compute now rebuilds on a price-distillate refresh (snapshot.py:262) and on dropped symbols (snapshot.py:272), GLEIF refreshes weekly instead of freezing (service.py:322,477), FSDS drops co-registrant rows (edgar_fsds.py:90) and streams. But the same compute path now mis-aligns price/return_6m to the wrong fiscal period whenever a scraped symbol carries deep audited history (reconcile.py:63 + snapshot.py:79/96) — a NEW P1 on the enabled deep-history path.

Broad green suite (221+62) with the new sources now covered by representative fixtures; test-first discipline is visible. The one gap is the order-blind F6 reconcile fixture that missed the regression.
Unchanged; F5/c3 mirror fetch still uncapped (mirror.py:102,88), trusted URLs only.
Provider parsers refactored into small streaming helpers (edgar_fsds.py, gleif.py); enrichment.py still concentrates 7 cycles in 617 LOC (F5/c3).
Bulk archives streamed (gleif.py, edgar_fsds.py iter_fsds) — the OOM class that threatened the self-hosted target is gone; incremental compute still recomputes only dirty symbols.

topFindings: F1 (P1): reconcile.py:63 appends audited-only periods unsorted → snapshot.py:79/96 write price & return_6m to the wrong (old) period for every scraped symbol with FSDS/EDGAR deep history; latest-period ratios and value/momentum ranks go stale/NaN.
Deep-history feature now RETAINS the periods (F6 truncation fixed) but mis-prices them — the fix solved data loss and introduced data corruption on the same path.
F2/c3 (P2): audited-only symbols carry no field-level provenance (snapshot.py:199 passes audited_frames=None).

author: judge-reliability-c3

dimensionScores.rationale: Ten c2 findings verified fixed in code with tests; the OOM and both silent P1s (truncation, UK zero-rows) are gone. Held below clearing by one NEW silent P1: the F6 append is unsorted (reconcile.py:63) so the current price/momentum attach to a historical period (snapshot.py:79,96) — silent, test-invisible, and it contradicts the very feature the fix restored.

221 pytest + 62 vitest green; the added fixtures are representative (real UK filename, coreg, FX latest-only, price refresh) — a clear improvement — but a green suite again hides a P1 because the F6 fixture never asserts period order.
Flat; only F5/c3 mirror byte/time cap outstanding (mirror.py:102,88).
Cleaner provider internals; enrichment.py 617-LOC hotspot unchanged (F5/c3).
Streaming bulk parsers remove the OOM risk on the 1GB-VPS target — the main reliability-relevant perf gain.

topFindings: Recovery is real: the two c2 P1s and the P2 correctness cluster are genuinely fixed and the OOM is closed — the core is stronger than the 73 baseline.

F1 (P1): a new, silent, test-invisible regression from the F6 fix mis-aligns latest-period price/return_6m for the deep-history universe (reconcile.py:63; snapshot.py:79,96; repro runs/regression-price-period.txt) — below the 80 bar until it is fixed.
No P0; residual P2s are provenance (F2/c3) and opt-in EDINET (F3/F4).
